whatChanged: Added an AIOS-owned, read-only codebase map proof for future Codex, Claude, Harlan, and build-agent sessions.
whatItDoes: Scans approved repo surfaces, classifies files by category and product surface, extracts markdown headings plus JavaScript import/export edges, flags large-file risk, and rejects private/local/archive/generated graph leaks.
whyItMatters: Long Codex sessions and handoffs need a fast way to understand the codebase without loading private memory, installing third-party graph plugins, committing generated graphs, or guessing from stale chat context.
whereItLives: lib/agentic-codebase-map.js read-only scanner, evaluator, and dogfood proof
proofStatus: Focused proof maps 2,575 approved files, confirms 13/13 critical surfaces, surfaces 7 size-risk flags, rejects private/local paths, rejects archive inclusion, rejects generated graph paths, and confirms no install, symlink, subprocess, or write path in the repo-map module.
reviewNext: Use this as the local AIOS-owned codebase map layer for future agent sessions. Continue SOURCE-BROWSER-AGENTIC-RUNTIME-001 and AGENT-TEMPLATE-RUNTIME-CONTRACT-001 for live browser/source-session and reusable agent runtime work. If generated graph artifacts or a dashboard view are needed, scope a separate artifact-policy card before committing generated output.
knownLimits: This is a read-only codebase map proof, not a live source-browser, extractor, or Harlan runtime.
This does not install Understand-Anything, Graphify, Browserbase, Stagehand, or any third-party plugin.
This does not commit generated knowledge graphs, vector stores, screenshots, transcripts, or runtime artifacts.
This does not scan private/local-only workspace state such as memory, MEMORY.md, USER.md, TOOLS.md, IDENTITY.md, HEARTBEAT.md, .claude, .openclaw, .env, or node_modules.
This does not call providers/models, run live extraction, open browsers, mutate credentials, write external systems, or grant agents new authority.

acceptanceState: Verified Harlan Telegram live-delivery preflight is accepted as blocked-preflight, not done; it can prepare Steve-only Telegram request packets with sendsMessageNow=false, while live Telegram send, bot creation, source-session resume, login, signup, paid/private extraction, posting, messaging, downloads, credential mutation, and broad Harlan runtime remain approval-bound continuation work.
whatChanged: Added the Harlan Telegram live-delivery preflight contract, focused proof, approval record, handoff, and current-doc readback.
whatItDoes: Turns an auth-needed Harlan escalation into a Telegram delivery packet that is Steve-only, Telegram-only, metadata-ref-only, deduped by issue, DONE-gated, reverify-before-resume, and fail-closed when config or approval is missing. It can prepare an approved-live-test request preview, but proof paths keep sendsMessageNow=false and externalSent=false.
whyItMatters: God Mode source-browser and source-session work needs a real operator escalation lane before Skool, MyICOR, newsletter, and auth-gated runs can be trusted overnight. This slice removes ambiguity around Telegram readiness without quietly enabling a live external sender.
whereItLives: lib/harlan-auth-live-delivery.js Telegram delivery preflight contract, config resolver, request packet builder, evaluator, and dogfood proof
scripts/process-harlan-auth-live-delivery-check.mjs focused proof for approval, Plan Critic, dogfood, no-network-sender, closeout, and docs
proofStatus: Focused proof validates approval, Plan Critic, live backlog continuation card, Harlan Telegram contract, missing-config blocked-preflight behavior, approved-config request preview with no send, raw secret rejection, duplicate suppression, DONE/reverify/resume, timeout fail-closed, non-Steve target rejection, no Telegram network sender implementation, package script, closeout registry, current docs, handoff, and module-size guardrails.
reviewNext: When Steve is ready, configure and approve the exact Telegram bot/token secret ref and Steve chat ID secret ref, then run one Steve-only live test. Continue Source Browser Agent and Source Session Broker work with Harlan packets still no-send until that approval lands. Broader Harlan runtime remains HARLAN-LIVE-OPERATOR-RUNTIME-002.
knownLimits: This is blocked-preflight and not done as live Harlan auth delivery.
This does not send live Telegram, create a Telegram bot, call Telegram APIs, or implement a network sender.
This does not store or print raw Telegram bot tokens or raw chat IDs.
This does not log in, sign up, resume source sessions, solve challenges, buy, download, post, comment, message, mutate profiles, mutate credentials, or use a normal Chrome profile.
This does not launch broad Harlan live runtime, UI, voice, avatar, inbound Telegram bots, or external-action authority; continue HARLAN-LIVE-OPERATOR-RUNTIME-002 for that layer.

whatChanged: Added the Foundation-owned Harlan auth escalation loop contract and focused dry-run proof.
whatItDoes: Turns provider, extractor, or Harlan `auth_needed` into a `blocked-auth` event, prepares a Steve-only Harlan-on-Telegram notification draft, dedups duplicate issue keys, waits for `DONE`, silently re-verifies, resumes only after proof, and fails closed on timeout or failed reverify.
whyItMatters: Brain Fleet and extractor work need a governed auth-needed path before live provider probes, paid/private sources, Skool/MyICOR access, or overnight extraction scale can be trusted.
proofStatus: Focused proof validates approval, Plan Critic, live backlog/current sprint truth, old-system source harvest, auth_needed -> blocked-auth, dedup/no spam, timeout/fail-closed, DONE/retry/resume, no credential mutation, dry-run no-send behavior, package script, verifier coverage, closeout registry, current plan/state, and full Foundation ship gates.
reviewNext: Continue BRAIN-FLEET-QUOTA-LEDGER-001 before live provider probes, route capability probes, or extractor runtime proof.
knownLimits: This card does not send live Telegram, email, Gmail, Slack, ClickUp, Drive, or Agent Feedback messages.
This card does not mutate credentials, OAuth tokens, browser profiles, provider config, llm_credentials, llm_routes, source systems, Drive permissions, or external systems.
This card does not run live provider probes, model calls, paid/private source access, browser automation, broad crawls, or extractor runtime.
This card does not build Strategy, People, broad Skool/MyICOR/Loom extraction, quota ledger, capability registry, provider adapters, OpenClaw boundary, or extractor proof work.

whatChanged: Added the Foundation-owned agent usefulness gate bundle and focused dogfood proof.

whatChanged: Added the Foundation-up provider/tool capability registry and fail-closed focused proof.
whatItDoes: Registers Fal image generation, ElevenLabs voice, Canva read metadata, and local terminal worker capabilities before any agent or worker can claim/use them. Each row names owner, env refs by name only, permission class, cost policy, audit log, callable path, proof command, approval boundary, and blocked agent binding status.
whyItMatters: Provider tools and local workers must become Foundation-governed capabilities before Harlan, Crewbert, role assistants, or builders can drift into hidden authority, provider spend, unclear ownership, or side-effect lanes.
whereItLives: lib/foundation-up-capability-registry.js registry contract, evaluator, report renderer, and dogfood proof
